@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly finding Amy Jones’s author_id and a long list of her article_ids, the agent only classified the 5 articles returned by its Mongo query (var_call_8bcqORZF9Zuz8tcuT3RKSssi) and computed 3/5 = 0.6, thus failing to include all her articles in the calculation.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly finding Amy Jones’s author_id and many article_ids, the agent’s Mongo query to the articles collection returned only five documents (var_call_1icJZShRRJoAxbsuWaG9q3zn), and it computed the fraction from just those five (3/5) rather than all articles, indicating it failed to retrieve/process the full result set.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Using the correct article IDs and texts (from query_db calls) the agent’s execute_python step applied a simplistic keyword-based classifier with a tie-breaker biased toward “Science/Technology,” leading to one misclassification and an overcount (17/111 instead of the ground-truth 16/111).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving Amy Jones’s 111 articles (via authors, article_metadata, and articles queries), the agent used a naive keyword-based classifier in the execute_python step to assign categories, which misclassified several items and yielded 13/111 instead of the correct 16/111 fraction.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
